_metadata.title: Syllabus Completeness — Auditor's Reference
_metadata.status: v0.x untested — sandbox-validated only; promote to 1.0 after a real (non-sandbox) course run

scope: Whether a course syllabus is COMPLETE — contains the sections students need. A completeness check, not a prose-quality judgment. Tone/clarity/'is each outcome assessed' are human judgments surfaced as advisory data, never pass/fail.
consumed_by: syllabus_audit.py (detection engine; reads syllabus_body via GET /courses/:id?include[]=syllabus_body)

required_sections.contains: name, office, office hours, email, phone

ai_policy_gate.requirement: Per BYU-Idaho AI hub, every course syllabus MUST include a statement about generative AI use. Treated as a required gate that can drive an 'incomplete' verdict on its own.

frameworks.AI Assessment Scale: No AI -> AI for Ideas -> AI for Feedback -> AI for Content -> AI-Led

ai_policy_gate.provenance: Five free-text option statements (No AI / No AI Content Creation / Cite / Open / Case-by-Case) trace to James Helfrich; Stoplight framework is the BYU-I AI Task Force's. Detection-vs-declaration evidence: AI detectors are unreliable, so a clear operationalized policy beats policing (pre_knowledge byui_ai_agency.md).

advisory_signals: syllabus bloat (pitfall #1) — very high word count is a flag, not a failure
vague expectations (pitfall #2) / dry-contractual tone (pitfall #3) — human judgments, out of deterministic scope
outcomes stated — present in syllabus text (deeper mapped+assessed check is outcomes_quality territory)
facts.detection_caveat: Section detection is keyword-heuristic on stripped syllabus HTML. 'Not detected' means REVIEW, not proven-absent (same evidence-based posture as rubric_quality_audit.py Criterion 1). The audit surfaces data for a human decision; it does not certify deficiency.

provenance: BYU-Idaho Campus Curriculum Development syllabus template (9 required sections) — pre_knowledge/byui_learning_teaching/byui_syllabus_guidance.md
BYU-Idaho AI hub 'AI in the Syllabus' (byui.edu/ai/academics/ai-in-the-syllabus) — pre_knowledge/byui_learning_teaching/byui_ai_hub.md
General higher-ed syllabus practice (instructor contact, outcomes, materials, grading, accessibility, academic integrity)

validation.evidence: 16/16 logic checks on synthetic syllabi; live read against CANVAS_SANDBOX_ID (145706) returned 5/9 sections on a real 293-word syllabus_body; exit codes (0/1/2) + --json confirmed 2026-05-22. NOT yet exercised against a real non-sandbox course — hence v0.1 and not yet in knowledge/README.md catalog.

changes: Created from the BYUI Learning & Teaching harvest (syllabus template + byui.edu/ai). Grounds syllabus_audit.py. Institution-neutral checklist with BYUI as the anchor profile; BYUI-specific items (Learning Model, Vision) flagged. Wired into canvas_course_expert.json with a v0.x-untested caveat. Held out of knowledge/README.md catalog per the v0.x->v1.0 convention (promote after a real-course run).
